_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that different types of texts across all our school subjects have different structures and language features depending on what they are trying to do.

_source.w: We are learning that different types of texts across all our school subjects have different structures and language features depending on what they are trying to do.
y: Recognising text structures helps us read more efficiently across all subjects.
Understanding how texts are built helps us create well-organised writing of our own.
This knowledge helps us switch confidently between different types of writing.
eg: We compare a science report with a history recount and identify their different features.
We describe how a procedure text is structured differently from an explanation text.
We identify the language features of persuasive texts — like emotive words and rhetorical questions.
We look at how information texts use headings, subheadings and diagrams to organise ideas.
We match text types to their purposes and discuss why the structure suits the purpose.
We plan our own writing by first identifying the text type and its expected structure.
